# idasen

{{ PyPi Version }} {{ Build Status }} {{ Documentation Status }} {{ Black }}

The IDÅSEN is an electric sitting standing desk with a Linak controller sold by
ikea.

The position of the desk can controlled by a physical switch on the desk or
via bluetooth using an phone app.

This is a command line interface written in python to control the Idasen via
bluetooth from a desktop computer.

## Set Up

### Prerequisites

The desk should be connected to the computer.

### Install

```bash
python3 -m pip install --upgrade idasen
```

## Configuration

Configuration that is not expected to change frequently can be provided via a
YAML configuration file located at `~/.config/idasen/idasen.yaml`.

You can use this command to initialize a new configuartion file:

```bash
idasen init
```

```yaml
mac_address: AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA
positions:
    sit: 0.75
    stand: 1.1
```

Configuartion options:

- `mac_address` - The MAC address of the desk. This is required.
- `positions` - A dictionary of positions with values of desk height from the
  floor in meters, `sit` and `stand` are provided as examples.

The program will try to discover the device address. The device needs to be in
'discover' mode for this to work.

If discovery fails the device MAC addresses can be found using `blueoothctl`
and bluetooth adapter names can be found with `hcitool dev` on linux.

If the device has not yet been paired, this can be accomplished with:

```bash
idasen pair
```

Again, the device will need to be in 'discover' mode for this to work.

## Usage

### Command Line

To print the current desk height:

```bash
idasen height
```

To monitor for changes to height:

```bash
idasen monitor
```

To save the current height as the sitting position:

```bash
idasen save sit
```

To delete the saved sitting position:

```bash
idasen delete sit
```

Assuming the config file is populated to move the desk to sitting position:

```bash
idasen sit
```

functions.name: idasen.cli.load_config
documentation: Load user config.

functions.name: idasen._bytes_to_meters_and_speed
documentation: Converts a value read from the desk in bytes to height in meters and speed.
height calculation offset in meters, assumed to be the same for all desks

functions.name: idasen._meters_to_bytes
documentation: Converts meters to bytes for setting the position on the desk

classes.name: idasen._DeskLoggingAdapter
documentation: Prepends logging messages with the desk MAC address.

classes.name: idasen.IdasenDesk

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.pair
documentation:
Pair with the desk.

This method is not available on macOS. Instead of manually initiating
paring, the user will be prompted to pair automatically as soon as it
is required.

See {py:meth}`bleak.BleakClient.pair` for more information.

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.connect
documentation:
Connect to the desk.

This method is an alternative to the context manager.
When possible the context manager is preferred.

```pycon
>>> async def example() -> bool:
...     desk = IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA")
...     await desk.connect()  # don't forget to call disconnect later!
...     return desk.is_connected
>>> asyncio.run(example())
True
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.disconnect
documentation:
Disconnect from the desk.

This method is an alternative to the context manager.
When possible the context manager is preferred.

```pycon
>>> async def example() -> bool:
...     desk = IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA")
...     await desk.connect()
...     await desk.disconnect()
...     return desk.is_connected
>>> asyncio.run(example())
False
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.is_connected
documentation:
`True` if the desk is connected.

```pycon
>>> async def example() -> bool:
...     async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
...         return desk.is_connected
>>> asyncio.run(example())
True
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.is_moving
documentation: ``True`` if the desk is currently being moved by this class.

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.mac
documentation:
Desk MAC address.

```pycon
>>> async def example() -> str:
...     async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
...         return desk.mac
>>> asyncio.run(example())
'AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA'
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.wakeup
documentation:
Wakeup the controller from sleep.

This exists for compatibility with the Linak DPG1C controller,
it is not necessary with the original idasen controller.

```pycon
>>> async def example():
...     async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
...         await desk.wakeup()
>>> asyncio.run(example())
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.move_up
documentation:
Move the desk upwards.

This command moves the desk upwards for a fixed duration
(approximately one second) as set by your desk controller.

```pycon
>>> async def example():
...     async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
...         await desk.move_up()
>>> asyncio.run(example())
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.move_down
documentation:
Move the desk downwards.

This command moves the desk downwards for a fixed duration
(approximately one second) as set by your desk controller.

```pycon
>>> async def example():
...     async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
...         await desk.move_down()
>>> asyncio.run(example())
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.move_to_target
documentation:
Move the desk to the target position.

Args:

: target: Target position in meters.

Raises:

: ValueError: Target exceeds maximum or minimum limits.

```pycon
>>> async def example():
...     async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
...         await desk.move_to_target(1.1)
>>> asyncio.run(example())
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.stop
documentation: Stop desk movement.

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk._stop
documentation: Send stop commands

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.get_height
documentation:
Get the desk height in meters.

Returns:

: Desk height in meters.

```pycon
>>> async def example() -> float:
...     async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
...         await desk.move_to_target(1.0)
...         return await desk.get_height()
>>> asyncio.run(example())
1.0
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.get_speed
documentation:
Get the desk speed in meters per second.

Returns:

: Desk speed in meters per second.

```pycon
>>> async def example() -> float:
...     async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
...         await desk.move_to_target(1.0)
...         return await desk.get_speed()
>>> asyncio.run(example())
0.0
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.get_height_and_speed
documentation:
Get the desk height in meters and speed in meters per second.

Returns:

: Tuple of desk height in meters and speed in meters per second.

```pycon
>>> async def example() -> [float, float]:
...     async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
...         await desk.move_to_target(1.0)
...         return await desk.get_height_and_speed()
>>> asyncio.run(example())
(1.0, 0.0)
```

methods.name: idasen.IdasenDesk.discover
documentation:
Try to find the desk's MAC address by discovering currently connected devices.

Returns:

: MAC address if found, `None` if not found.

```pycon
>>> asyncio.run(IdasenDesk.discover())
'AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA'
```

Idasen desk.

Args:

: mac: Bluetooth MAC address of the desk, or an instance of a BLEDevice.
  exit_on_fail: If set to True, failing to connect will call `sys.exit(1)`,

  > otherwise the exception will be raised.

  disconnected_callback:

  : Callback that will be scheduled in the event loop when the client is
    disconnected. The callable must take one argument, which will be
    this client object.

Note:

: There is no locking to prevent you from running multiple movement
  coroutines simultaneously.

Example:

: Basic Usage:

  ```
  from idasen import IdasenDesk


  async with IdasenDesk(mac="AA:AA:AA:AA:AA:AA") as desk:
      # call methods here...
  ```
